content.problemStatement: You are given a sequence **a1, a2, ..., aN**. Find the smallest possible value of **ai + aj**, where 1 ≤ **i** < **j** ≤ **N**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N, the number of elements in the sequence. The second line of each test case contains N space-separated integers - a1, a2, ..., aN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer - the smallest possible sum for the corresponding test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the smallest possible sum is 1 + 2 = 3. For the second test case, the smallest possible sum is 3 + 4 = 7.

solution.approach: The key insight is to sort the array in ascending order. The smallest possible sum of two elements will be the sum of the first two elements in the sorted array. This is because the smallest two elements will always be the first two elements in the sorted array. The time complexity of this approach is O(N log N) due to the sorting operation.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N) per test case, O(T * N log N) total.

code.Python3:
import sys

T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    arr.sort()
    print(arr[0] + arr[1])
